What does it really need to be the best that you could be? What is it, what's your responsibility as an adult to put food on the table, to provide shelter for your family, to go out and live a good life so you can do the things that you want, remember this. People have this mistaken notion, like don't you know, you really should, you gotta be passionate about what you do. You know, okay, that's real great, I sell toilet bowls. How do you get passionate about that? Or I'm a financial guy, I manage money. How do you really get passionate? Oh, I love managing money. Come on, that's a loaded. You don't love managing money, you love the fact that you make a fortune managing money. Yes, here's the bottom line. You might not love selling toilet bowls, okay, if you're a toilet bowl salesman, or you're a plumber, or whatever it is you sell, it's very rarely someone inspired by directly what they do. If you can be, that's awesome, but many times you can't be, it's disingenuous, and it's a bad thing to say that you are. What can always be inspired about is helping people get what they want, and also getting you what you want. You need to start linking up, this week I want you to focus on linking up that the work I do every single day translates to more money in my pocket, and hence a better lifestyle. That's why it's so important to have that vision to the future, clearly thought out, that fully integrated vision of the future which are why attached, to know why making money is important to you. And I want you to really, if you don't have it, put it on your board, you know, every one out, or it's not working for you, rewrite it, pump it up, make it, just so it hits you viscerally, and put it on your phone screensaver. Let's make this vision week, where you look at your vision every day, I want you to step in, integrate yourself into your vision. Remember, there's two types of ways that we can imagine ourselves in the future having succeeded. The first way is called a dissociated state. A dissociated state means that you are looking down almost if it was in a movie, and you see yourself in a picture in the future, living the life you want and feeling great. You actually motivate yourself by seeing yourself from above and saying, wow, here's how my life is gonna be, here's where I'm wearing, here's where I'm living, here's what my kids do, here's how I see myself smiling, living the life I want, traveling, that's called dissociated. Where you actually look down and see yourself. The second way is through an associated state, meaning that you're actually step inside your own body in the future and as if you're looking out for your own eyes and seeing yourself, seeing the world around you through your own eyes. Now, when it comes to getting an emotional feeling inside, to really build up the emotion of feeling great, being in an associated state is more powerful. We actually, inside your own body, it's not always easy to do, but you have to practice that, right? And we've built exercises, maybe one of the weekly intensive, I'll do that, right? But the point is, it's not the easiest to do, but you can do it with practice, where you imagine yourself inside your own body, seeing the world through your own eyes in the future and everything is the way you want it to be. That's important to create the emotion, but believe it or not, when it comes to motivation, to motivate yourself, the dissociated state is the more powerful, the more effective one, to actually be looking down at your own life five years from now and imagine yourself and see yourself looking good, feeling good, living good, and your vision is now a reality. And then you backtrack, and I want you then to start linking up the day-to-day effort that you put into things and how you can draw a direct line from the work you do today to where you're going to be in five years. Now, it's not always easy to do, but with practice, you can do it. It starts by just doing it every day for a few minutes, just really linking up, say, you know, John, Jordan, Jin, Jane, right? Every, say to yourself, what I'm doing right now, it is a direct line to my future. You know, it's so funny to say, you know, the future, well, the future doesn't, you know, the future, the future, the future, right? Bottom line, the future is coming, whether you believe it or not, whether you want it or not, and the work you do today, how much effort you put into the things that you do, it's going to be directly related to where you end up. We live in a world of cause and effect, we just do. The actions that you take today, the decisions that you make, are going to lead you down a certain path. Now, the goals that you set, right? You've spoken as many times, the goals that you set, serve as these stein posts along the way, to show whether you're moving in the right direction or wrong direction. But at the end of the day, it's that vision to your future, that's where your emotional attachment is, that you'll just do anything that's necessary to get there. I'm a whatever it takes person. I see myself in the future, living awesome, living the exact life I want, taking care of the people I love, my family, my parents, the causes I believe in. Make sure that your why is integrated into your vision and includes not just your own greed, your own wants, but those that you love and care about. That's where your power lies. You get, I always tell the story about how I was in jail and when it kept me going, it was at night time when I was alone with my thoughts, I would close my eyes and I would see the faces of my two children. And that was what my why was. And that allowed me to stay positive no matter what. But what's your why? Seriously, what is your why? Have you identified it? Is it on your phone screen? Seriously, if you're having problems, getting yourself to do what you know you have to do every single day, day in and day out, if you haven't promised that, it's because you don't have your why. It's not it there, you're not using your why. I promise you that when you have a clear vision to the future and you know your why, you will be motivated. And I want you to start linking up the actions you take today, this entire week, it's vision week. I want you to just go at like future pacing, act as if. No, this term right, right? I made it clear, act as if. They took it from me in a boiler room, right? Which is the guy who works for me. Act as if you're a wealthy man rich already and you'll become rich. And I didn't invent acting as if, just see, you know, but I was the one that came up with the line, act as if you're a wealthy man, right? From boiler room, that was me, right? Act as if you're an unmatched confidence and you'll become confident. Act as if you have only answers to the answer. Come to act as if.
